headline: Digital Divide Leaves 8.5 Million Without Reliable Internet
context: MCMC estimates 8.5 million Malaysians lack reliable internet access sufficient for basic digital services.

cards.big: 8.5 million Malaysians lack reliable internet for basic digital services
cards.sub: Rural and B40 communities increasingly excluded from digitizing economy

cards.big: Fixed broadband in B40 households is 42% versus 92% for T20
cards.sub: Monthly RM120 cost consumes 8% of B40 income versus 1.2% of T20

cards.big: 28% of students relied on mobile-only internet for education in 2025
cards.sub: Data caps of 30GB monthly insufficient for video learning platforms

cards.big: JENDELA achieved 95% 4G coverage but speed below 10 Mbps in 35% of areas
cards.sub: Coverage claims mask quality issues making services practically unusable

cards.big: Digital divide tests whether connectivity statistics reflect actual usability for citizens
8.5 million without reliable internet. The digital divide is an infrastructure deficit with economic consequences.
